# James Keywood

## Identity

James Keywood is a BSc Computer Science with Industrial Experience undergraduate at The University of Manchester (2023–2027). He is a software engineer with experience in backend development, data engineering, machine learning, and software projects.

Location: Manchester, England, United Kingdom

Professional profiles:
- Portfolio: https://jameskeywood.github.io/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jameskeywood

## Professional Summary

Computer Science undergraduate with first-class academic performance and industry experience as a Software Engineer at Nomura. Experienced in building software systems, data platforms, machine learning applications, and developer tools.

## Education

The University of Manchester
- Degree: BSc Computer Science with Industrial Experience
- Duration: September 2023 – June 2027
- Academic performance:
  - Year 1: First-class (79%)
  - Year 2: First-class (76%)

Kingston Grammar School
- A-levels:
  - Computer Science: A*
  - Mathematics: A*
  - Further Mathematics: A*
  - Physics: A*
- EPQ: Explored Generative Adversarial Networks

## Industry Experience

### Software Engineer — Nomura
June 2025 – June 2026

Worked on the Unity Data Warehouse team supporting firm-wide risk analytics.

Responsibilities:
- Maintained trade and risk data warehouse systems.
- Planned and executed platform upgrades.
- Delivered architectural migrations.
- Worked with enterprise data technologies.

Technologies:
- Java
- Dremio
- MinIO
- Apache Iceberg
- Apache Spark
- ETL pipelines
- Maven
- GitLab CI/CD
- DevOps tooling

### Software Engineer Intern — Goldclipper Computing Limited
June 2021 – July 2021

Developed an internal application using Oracle Application Express.

Responsibilities:
- Built employee timesheet tracking functionality.
- Implemented payroll calculation features.

Technologies:
- Oracle APEX
- PL/SQL

## Selected Projects

### Tig

A Git companion tool developed during GreatUniHack 2024.

Description:
Built a developer tool allowing files to be attached to Git commits and visualised alongside repository history.

Technologies:
- Go
- Gin

Achievement:
- Designed and implemented a working prototype within a 24-hour hackathon.

### Starmapper

Machine learning web application for identifying stars and clusters from uploaded images.

Description:
Built a constellation classification system using computer vision and machine learning.

Technologies:
- Python
- TensorFlow
- OpenCV
- Flask

Achievement:
- Classified 16 constellations using a custom machine learning pipeline.

### lightr

Web application project involving:
- PHP
- JavaScript
- CSS
- SQLite
- Chart.js
